Tests for the timeline peek feature in the Pebble firmware, including setup, rendering, and visibility.

# Purpose
The code is a test suite for the `timeline_peek` functionality, which is part of a graphical user interface system. It includes setup and teardown functions, helper functions, and a series of test cases to verify the behavior of the timeline peek feature. The code uses a combination of fake and stubbed components to simulate the environment in which the timeline peek operates. This includes fake real-time clock (`fake_rtc`) and SPI flash (`fake_spi_flash`) components, as well as various stubbed services like `stubs_activity` and `stubs_analytics`.

The test suite initializes the environment by setting up time, graphics context, and resources. It then defines several test cases to check the rendering and visibility of timeline peek items under different conditions. The tests cover scenarios such as rendering items with different titles, subtitles, and icons, as well as checking the visibility of the timeline peek when transitioning in and out of a watchface context. The code uses helper functions to render layers and set timeline items, and it verifies the expected outcomes using assertions.

# Global Variables

---
### s\_ctx
- **Type**: ``GContext``
- **Description**: Represents a static instance of the `GContext` structure, which is used for managing graphics context in the application. It is initialized with a framebuffer and is used to render graphics on the display.
- **Use**: Used to provide a graphics context for rendering operations in the application.


---
### s\_is\_watchface\_running
- **Type**: ``bool``
- **Description**: Indicates if the watchface is currently running. It is a static boolean variable, meaning it is only accessible within the file it is declared in.
- **Use**: Used to check the running state of the watchface in the `app_manager_is_watchface_running` function.


---
### s\_fb
- **Type**: ``FrameBuffer``
- **Description**: Represents a static instance of the `FrameBuffer` structure. This variable is used to manage and manipulate the graphical frame buffer for rendering operations.
- **Use**: Used to initialize and clear the frame buffer for graphical operations in the `test_timeline_peek__initialize` function.


---
### s\_dest\_bitmap
- **Type**: ``GBitmap *``
- **Description**: A pointer to a `GBitmap` structure, which represents a bitmap image in memory. The `GBitmap` structure is used to manage image data for rendering graphics.
- **Use**: Used to store and manage the destination bitmap for rendering operations in the graphics context.


# Data Structures

---
### TimelinePeekItemConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `timestamp`: Stores the time associated with the timeline item.
    - `title`: Holds the main text or heading for the timeline item.
    - `subtitle`: Contains additional descriptive text for the timeline item.
    - `icon`: Represents the icon resource identifier for the timeline item.
    - `num_concurrent`: Indicates the number of concurrent timeline items.
- **Description**: Defines the configuration for a timeline peek item, including its timestamp, title, subtitle, icon, and the number of concurrent items. This structure is used to manage and display timeline items in a user interface, providing essential information such as the time of the event, its title and subtitle, and an associated icon.

### clock\_get\_until\_time<!-- {{#callable:clock_get_until_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L45>)

Formats a buffer with the string 'In 5 minutes'.
- **Inputs**:
    - `buffer`: A pointer to a character array where the formatted string will be stored.
    - `buf_size`: The size of the buffer to ensure the formatted string does not exceed this size.
    - `timestamp`: A `time_t` value representing a specific point in time, though it is not used in the function.
    - `max_relative_hrs`: An integer representing the maximum relative hours, though it is not used in the function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Uses `snprintf` to write the string 'In 5 minutes' into the `buffer`.
    - Limits the written string to `buf_size` to prevent buffer overflow.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `buffer` in place.

### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_initialize<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__initialize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L115>)

Initializes the test environment for timeline peek functionality by setting up time, graphics context, resources, and initializing the timeline peek.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set the timezone to UTC and update the RTC time to the start of the day.
    - Initialize the fake RTC to simulate a specific start time of 5pm on January 1, 2015.
    - Initialize the framebuffer and graphics context for rendering.
    - Set up the fake SPI flash and initialize the filesystem, then load resource fixtures into flash.
    - Set the watchface running state to true and initialize the timeline peek.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`time_util_update_timezone`](<../../../src/fw/util/time/time.c.md#time_util_update_timezone>)
    - [`mktime`](<../../../src/fw/util/time/mktime.c.md#mktime>)
    - [`fake_rtc_init`](<../../fakes/fake_rtc.c.md#fake_rtc_init>)
    - [`fake_spi_flash_init`](<../../fakes/fake_spi_flash.c.md#fake_spi_flash_init>)
    - [`pfs_init`](<../../../src/fw/services/normal/filesystem/pfs.c.md#pfs_init>)
    - [`pfs_format`](<../../../src/fw/services/normal/filesystem/pfs.c.md#pfs_format>)
    - [`load_resource_fixture_in_flash`](<../../fixtures/load_test_resources.h.md#load_resource_fixture_in_flash>)
    - [`resource_init`](<../../../src/fw/resource/resource.c.md#resource_init>)

### prv\_render\_layer<!-- {{#callable:prv_render_layer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L160>)

Renders a `Layer` into a bitmap, optionally using the screen dimensions.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` to render.
    - ``box``: A pointer to a `GRect` defining the area to render.
    - ``use_screen``: A boolean indicating whether to use the screen dimensions for rendering.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Destroy the existing destination bitmap `s_dest_bitmap`.
    - Determine the `drawing_box` based on `use_screen`; use `DISP_FRAME` if true, otherwise use `box`.
    - Create a blank bitmap `s_dest_bitmap` with the size of `drawing_box`.
    - Set the destination bitmap and drawing parameters in the global graphics context `s_ctx`.
    - Render the `layer` into the graphics context `s_ctx`.
    - If `use_screen` is true, adjust the bounds of `screen_bitmap`, create a new blank bitmap for `s_dest_bitmap`, copy the contents of `screen_bitmap` into `s_dest_bitmap`, and destroy `screen_bitmap`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies global state by updating `s_dest_bitmap` and `s_ctx`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_render_tree`](<../../../src/fw/applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_render_tree>)
    - [`gpoint_neg`](<../../../src/fw/applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_neg>)


---
### prv\_set\_timeline\_item<!-- {{#callable:prv_set_timeline_item}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L191>)

Creates and sets a `TimelineItem` based on the provided configuration and updates the timeline peek display.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``config``: A pointer to a `TimelinePeekItemConfig` structure containing the configuration for the timeline item, including timestamp, title, subtitle, icon, and number of concurrent items.
    - ``animated``: A boolean indicating whether the timeline peek should be animated when set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `item` to `NULL` and get the current time using `rtc_get_time()`.
    - Determine the `timestamp` to use, defaulting to the current time if `config` is `NULL` or `config->timestamp` is zero.
    - If `config` is not `NULL`, initialize an `AttributeList` with three attributes.
    - Add the title, subtitle (if present), and icon to the `AttributeList`.
    - Create a `TimelineItem` with the specified attributes and destroy the `AttributeList` after use.
    - Set the timeline peek item using `timeline_peek_set_item()` with the created `TimelineItem`, a boolean indicating if the timestamp is in the future, the number of concurrent items, and the `animated` flag.
    - Return the created `TimelineItem`.
- **Output**: A pointer to the created `TimelineItem`, or `NULL` if no item was created.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`attribute_list_init_list`](<../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/attribute.c.md#attribute_list_init_list>)


---
### prv\_render\_timeline\_peek<!-- {{#callable:prv_render_timeline_peek}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L213>)

Renders a timeline peek item on the screen and ensures it is visible.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``config``: A pointer to a `TimelinePeekItemConfig` structure that contains configuration details for the timeline peek item.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with `config` and `false` for `animated` to create and set a timeline item.
    - Call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `true` and `false` for `animated` to make the timeline peek visible.
    - Retrieve the `TimelinePeek` object using [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>).
    - Get the `layout_layer` from the `TimelinePeek` object.
    - Determine if the whole screen is needed for text flow using `PBL_IF_ROUND_ELSE`.
    - Call [`prv_render_layer`](<#prv_render_layer>) to render the layer on the screen with the specified `GRect` and `use_screen` flag.
    - Destroy the timeline item using `timeline_item_destroy`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates on the timeline peek and rendering system.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)
    - [`prv_render_layer`](<#prv_render_layer>)
    - [`gpoint_neg`](<../../../src/fw/applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_neg>)


---
### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L232>)

Renders a timeline peek with specified configuration and checks if the resulting bitmap matches a test file.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_render_timeline_peek`](<#prv_render_timeline_peek>) with a `TimelinePeekItemConfig` structure that includes a title, subtitle, icon, and number of concurrent items.
    - Uses `cl_check` to verify if the rendered bitmap `s_dest_bitmap` is equal to `TEST_PBI_FILE`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_render_timeline_peek`](<#prv_render_timeline_peek>)
    - [`gbitmap_pbi_eq`](<../graphics/util.h.md#gbitmap_pbi_eq>)

### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek\_visibility<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek_visibility}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L332>)

Tests the visibility of the timeline peek UI by asserting its position on the screen before and after showing or hiding it.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with `NULL` and `false` to hide the timeline peek UI without animation.
    - Get the `TimelinePeek` object and its `layout_layer`.
    - Assert that the `layout_layer`'s `y` position is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is off-screen.
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with a `TimelinePeekItemConfig` to show the timeline peek UI without animation.
    - Assert that the `layout_layer`'s `y` position is less than `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is on-screen.
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with `NULL` and `false` again to hide the timeline peek UI without animation.
    - Assert that the `layout_layer`'s `y` position is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is off-screen again.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function uses assertions to verify the visibility state of the timeline peek UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)


---
### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek\_visible\_to\_hidden\_outside\_of\_watchface<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek_visible_to_hidden_outside_of_watchface}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L355>)

Tests the visibility transitions of a timeline peek item when moving in and out of the watchface context.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set a timeline item with specific configuration using [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) and store the returned `TimelineItem` pointer.
    - Retrieve the current `TimelinePeek` object and its associated `Layer`.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` position is less than `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is visible on-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `false` to simulate leaving the watchface and call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `false` to hide the peek.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` position is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is off-screen.
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with `NULL` and `true` for `animated` to hide the peek UI using the animated path, and assert the `Layer`'s `y` position remains off-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `true` to simulate returning to the watchface and call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `true` to show the peek.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` position is still greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it remains off-screen.
    - Destroy the `TimelineItem` using `timeline_item_destroy`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function performs assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)

### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek\_hidden\_to\_visible\_outside\_of\_watchface<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek_hidden_to_visible_outside_of_watchface}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L387>)

Tests the transition of a timeline peek from hidden to visible state when outside of the watchface.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set the timeline item to `NULL` without animation using [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>).
    - Retrieve the current `TimelinePeek` and its `layout_layer`.
    - Assert that the `layout_layer`'s `y` origin is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is off-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `false` to simulate transitioning away from the watchface.
    - Set the timeline peek visibility to `false` without animation using `timeline_peek_set_visible`.
    - Assert again that the `layout_layer`'s `y` origin is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`.
    - Create a new timeline item with animation using [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) and assert that the `layout_layer` remains off-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `true` to simulate transitioning back to the watchface.
    - Set the timeline peek visibility to `true` without animation using `timeline_peek_set_visible`.
    - Assert that the `layout_layer`'s `y` origin is less than `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is now on-screen.
    - Destroy the created timeline item using `timeline_item_destroy`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a test function that uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)

### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek\_visible\_leaving\_and\_entering\_watchface<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek_visible_leaving_and_entering_watchface}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L418>)

Tests the visibility transitions of a timeline peek when leaving and entering the watchface.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create a `TimelineItem` using [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with specific configuration parameters.
    - Retrieve the `TimelinePeek` object and its associated `Layer`.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` coordinate is less than `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is visible on-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `false` to simulate leaving the watchface and call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `false` to hide the peek.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` coordinate is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is off-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `true` to simulate returning to the watchface and call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `true` to show the peek.
    - Assert that the `Layer`'s `y` coordinate is less than `DISP_ROWS`, indicating it is visible on-screen again.
    - Destroy the `TimelineItem` to clean up.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function performs assertions to verify behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)

### test\_timeline\_peek\_\_peek\_hidden\_leaving\_and\_entering\_watchface<!-- {{#callable:test_timeline_peek__peek_hidden_leaving_and_entering_watchface}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../tests/fw/ui/test_timeline_peek.c#L444>)

Tests the visibility and position of the timeline peek when transitioning between watchface states.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>) with `NULL` and `true` for `animated` to initialize the timeline peek item.
    - Get the current timeline peek using [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>) and store its layout layer in `layer`.
    - Assert that the `layer` is off-screen by checking if `layer->frame.origin.y` is greater than or equal to `DISP_ROWS`.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `false` to simulate leaving the watchface.
    - Call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `false` and `false` for `animated` to hide the peek.
    - Assert that the `layer` is still off-screen.
    - Set `s_is_watchface_running` to `true` to simulate entering the watchface.
    - Call `timeline_peek_set_visible` with `true` and `false` for `animated` to show the peek.
    - Assert that the `layer` is visible but still off-screen.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this function is a test function that uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_timeline_item`](<#prv_set_timeline_item>)
    - [`timeline_peek_get_peek`](<../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek.c.md#timeline_peek_get_peek>)
